# рҹӨ– Agent Handoff Document

> **Purpose**: This document is the single source of truth for any AI agent taking over operation of this platform. If you are an AI agent resuming work, read this document **in full** before executing any actions. It contains everything you need to understand the system state, diagnose issues, recover from failures, and resume productive work.

**Platform**: AI-Powered Passive Income Generator  
**Products**: KDP Coloring Books В· Etsy Wall Art  
**Core Stack**: ComfyUI (image generation) В· Ollama (text/prompt generation) В· Python (orchestration)  
**Last Updated**: 2026-06-14

---

## Table of Contents

1. [System State Checklist](#1-system-state-checklist)
2. [How to Read Logs](#2-how-to-read-logs)
3. [How to Run Diagnostics](#3-how-to-run-diagnostics)
4. [Key File Locations](#4-key-file-locations)
5. [API Endpoints](#5-api-endpoints)
6. [Error Recovery Procedures](#6-error-recovery-procedures)
7. [Health Checks](#7-health-checks)
8. [Common Failure Modes and Fixes](#8-common-failure-modes-and-fixes)
9. [Resumption Protocol](#9-resumption-protocol)

---

## 1. System State Checklist

Before performing **any** work, verify every item below. If any check fails, refer to the [Error Recovery Procedures](#6-error-recovery-procedures) before proceeding.

| #  | Check                              | How to Verify                                                        | Expected Result              |
|----|-------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| 1  | ComfyUI server is running           | `curl http://127.0.0.1:8188/system_stats`                           | HTTP 200 with JSON stats      |
| 2  | Ollama server is running            | `curl http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags`                              | HTTP 200 with model list      |
| 3  | Required models are loaded          | Check Ollama model list and ComfyUI checkpoint list                  | All models present            |
| 4  | GPU is available                    | `nvidia-smi`                                                         | GPU listed, driver loaded     |
| 5  | VRAM is not exhausted               | `nvidia-smi` вҶ’ check memory usage                                   | Free VRAM > 2GB              |
| 6  | Disk space > 50GB free              | `Get-PSDrive D` (PowerShell)                                        | Free > 50GB                  |
| 7  | Output directories exist            | Check `D:\AI-Business\output\kdp\` and `D:\AI-Business\output\etsy\`| Directories present           |
| 8  | No stale lock files                 | `Get-ChildItem D:\AI-Business\ -Recurse -Filter "*.lock"`           | No results or only active locks|
| 9  | Log directory is writable           | `Test-Path D:\AI-Business\logs\`                                    | True                          |
| 10 | Python environment is ready         | `python --version` and check venv/packages                          | Python 3.10+                 |

### Quick Validation Script

```powershell
# Run all checks at once
Write-Host "=== System State Validation ===" -ForegroundColor Cyan

# 1. ComfyUI
try {
    $comfy = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://127.0.0.1:8188/system_stats" -TimeoutSec 5
    Write-Host "[PASS] ComfyUI is running" -ForegroundColor Green
} catch {
    Write-Host "[FAIL] ComfyUI is NOT running" -ForegroundColor Red
}

# 2. Ollama
try {
    $ollama = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags" -TimeoutSec 5
    Write-Host "[PASS] Ollama is running ($($ollama.models.Count) models loaded)" -ForegroundColor Green
} catch {
    Write-Host "[FAIL] Ollama is NOT running" -ForegroundColor Red
}

# 3. GPU
$gpu = & nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.free --format=csv,noheader 2>$null
if ($gpu) {
    Write-Host "[PASS] GPU: $gpu" -ForegroundColor Green
} else {
    Write-Host "[FAIL] GPU not detected" -ForegroundColor Red
}

# 4. Disk Space
$drive = Get-PSDrive D -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($drive -and ($drive.Free / 1GB) -gt 50) {
    Write-Host "[PASS] Disk space: $([math]::Round($drive.Free / 1GB, 1)) GB free" -ForegroundColor Green
} else {
    Write-Host "[FAIL] Disk space insufficient or drive not found" -ForegroundColor Red
}

# 5. Output directories
$dirs = @("D:\AI-Business\output\kdp", "D:\AI-Business\output\etsy", "D:\AI-Business\logs")
foreach ($d in $dirs) {
    if (Test-Path $d) {
        Write-Host "[PASS] Directory exists: $d" -ForegroundColor Green
    } else {
        Write-Host "[FAIL] Missing directory: $d" -ForegroundColor Red
    }
}

# 6. Stale lock files
$locks = Get-ChildItem "D:\AI-Business\" -Recurse -Filter "*.lock" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($locks) {
    Write-Host "[WARN] Found $($locks.Count) lock file(s):" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $locks | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "       $_" -ForegroundColor Yellow }
} else {
    Write-Host "[PASS] No stale lock files" -ForegroundColor Green
}
```

---

## 2. How to Read Logs

### Log Location

All logs are stored in `D:\AI-Business\logs\`.

### Log Files

| Log File                     | Purpose                                    | Rotation         |
|------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|------------------|
| `orchestrator.log`           | Main pipeline orchestration events         | Daily, 10MB max  |
| `comfyui_bridge.log`         | ComfyUI API interactions and results       | Daily, 10MB max  |
| `ollama_bridge.log`          | Ollama prompt/response logs                | Daily, 10MB max  |
| `pdf_assembly.log`           | PDF creation and validation                | Per-batch        |
| `listing_writer.log`         | Etsy/KDP listing text generation           | Per-batch        |
| `niche_research.log`         | Niche analysis and trend scoring           | Per-session      |
| `error.log`                  | Aggregated errors from all subsystems      | Daily, 50MB max  |
| `generation_history.jsonl`   | Structured record of all generations       | Append-only      |

### Log Format

```
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.mmm] [LEVEL] [MODULE] MESSAGE {optional_json_context}
```

**Example:**
```
[2026-06-14 12:34:56.789] [INFO] [orchestrator] Starting batch generation {"niche": "animals", "count": 24, "batch_id": "batch_20260614_001"}
[2026-06-14 12:35:01.234] [INFO] [comfyui_bridge] Image generated successfully {"prompt_id": "abc123", "duration_s": 4.2, "file": "animal_001.png"}
[2026-06-14 12:35:02.100] [ERROR] [comfyui_bridge] Generation failed {"prompt_id": "def456", "error": "CUDA out of memory", "retry": 1}
[2026-06-14 12:35:03.500] [WARN] [orchestrator] Retrying failed generation {"prompt_id": "def456", "attempt": 2, "max_retries": 3}
```

### Severity Levels

| Level     | Meaning                                           | Action Required?          |
|-----------|---------------------------------------------------|---------------------------|
| `DEBUG`   | Verbose diagnostic info (disabled in production)  | No                        |
| `INFO`    | Normal operation milestones                       | No                        |
| `WARN`    | Recoverable issue, operation continues            | Monitor вҖ” may escalate    |
| `ERROR`   | Operation failed, will retry or skip              | Investigate if recurring  |
| `CRITICAL`| System-level failure, pipeline halted             | Immediate intervention    |

### Useful Log Queries

```powershell
# Find all errors in the last hour
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\logs\error.log" -Tail 200 |
    Where-Object { $_ -match "\[ERROR\]|\[CRITICAL\]" }

# Find OOM errors
Select-String -Path "D:\AI-Business\logs\comfyui_bridge.log" -Pattern "out of memory|OOM"

# Get generation history for a specific niche
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\logs\generation_history.jsonl" |
    ConvertFrom-Json |
    Where-Object { $_.niche -eq "animals" }

# Count errors by module in the last 24h
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\logs\error.log" |
    Where-Object { $_ -match "^\[$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd')" } |
    ForEach-Object { if ($_ -match '\[(\w+)\].*\[(\w+)\]') { $Matches[2] } } |
    Group-Object | Sort-Object Count -Descending
```

---

## 3. How to Run Diagnostics

### Full System Diagnostic (Python)

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Full system diagnostic script.
Run: python D:\AI-Business\scripts\diagnostics.py
"""

import requests
import subprocess
import os
import json
import shutil
from datetime import datetime

def check_service(name, url, timeout=5):
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=timeout)
        return {"service": name, "status": "UP", "code": r.status_code, "response_ms": r.elapsed.total_seconds() * 1000}
    except Exception as e:
        return {"service": name, "status": "DOWN", "error": str(e)}

def check_gpu():
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["nvidia-smi", "--query-gpu=name,memory.total,memory.free,memory.used,utilization.gpu,temperature.gpu",
             "--format=csv,noheader,nounits"],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
        )
        parts = [p.strip() for p in result.stdout.strip().split(",")]
        return {
            "gpu_name": parts[0],
            "vram_total_mb": int(parts[1]),
            "vram_free_mb": int(parts[2]),
            "vram_used_mb": int(parts[3]),
            "gpu_utilization_pct": int(parts[4]),
            "temperature_c": int(parts[5])
        }
    except Exception as e:
        return {"error": str(e)}

def check_disk(path="D:\\"):
    total, used, free = shutil.disk_usage(path)
    return {
        "path": path,
        "total_gb": round(total / (1024**3), 1),
        "used_gb": round(used / (1024**3), 1),
        "free_gb": round(free / (1024**3), 1)
    }

def check_models():
    try:
        r = requests.get("http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags", timeout=5)
        models = [m["name"] for m in r.json().get("models", [])]
        return {"ollama_models": models, "count": len(models)}
    except:
        return {"ollama_models": [], "error": "Cannot reach Ollama"}

def check_lock_files():
    locks = []
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk("D:\\AI-Business"):
        for f in files:
            if f.endswith(".lock"):
                path = os.path.join(root, f)
                age_hours = (datetime.now().timestamp() - os.path.getmtime(path)) / 3600
                locks.append({"file": path, "age_hours": round(age_hours, 1)})
    return locks

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("=" * 60)
    print(f"  SYSTEM DIAGNOSTICS вҖ” {datetime.now().isoformat()}")
    print("=" * 60)

    # Services
    services = [
        check_service("ComfyUI", "http://127.0.0.1:8188/system_stats"),
        check_service("Ollama", "http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags"),
    ]
    print("\n--- Services ---")
    for s in services:
        status_icon = "вң…" if s["status"] == "UP" else "вқҢ"
        print(f"  {status_icon} {s['service']}: {s['status']}")

    # GPU
    print("\n--- GPU ---")
    gpu = check_gpu()
    print(f"  {json.dumps(gpu, indent=2)}")

    # Disk
    print("\n--- Disk ---")
    disk = check_disk()
    print(f"  Free: {disk['free_gb']} GB / {disk['total_gb']} GB")
    if disk["free_gb"] < 50:
        print("  вҡ пёҸ  WARNING: Less than 50GB free!")

    # Models
    print("\n--- Ollama Models ---")
    models = check_models()
    for m in models.get("ollama_models", []):
        print(f"  вҖў {m}")

    # Lock files
    print("\n--- Lock Files ---")
    locks = check_lock_files()
    if locks:
        for l in locks:
            print(f"  вҡ пёҸ  {l['file']} (age: {l['age_hours']}h)")
    else:
        print("  None found")

    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
```

### Quick curl Diagnostics

```bash
# ComfyUI system stats
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8188/system_stats | python -m json.tool

# ComfyUI object info (available nodes)
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8188/object_info | python -m json.tool | head -50

# ComfyUI queue status

# Ollama list loaded models

# Ollama test generation
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate -d '{"model":"llama3","prompt":"Say hello","stream":false}' | python -m json.tool

# GPU utilization (continuous monitoring)
nvidia-smi dmon -s u -d 1
```

### GPU Utilization Check

```powershell
# One-shot GPU check
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total,memory.free,utilization.gpu,temperature.gpu --format=csv

# Watch GPU every 2 seconds
while ($true) {
    $gpu = nvidia-smi --query-gpu=utilization.gpu,memory.used,memory.total --format=csv,noheader,nounits
    $parts = $gpu.Trim().Split(",") | ForEach-Object { $_.Trim() }
    Write-Host "[$(Get-Date -Format 'HH:mm:ss')] GPU: $($parts[0])% | VRAM: $($parts[1])/$($parts[2]) MB"
    Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
}
```

#### WebSocket вҖ” Real-time progress updates

```python
import websocket
import json

def on_message(ws, message):
    data = json.loads(message)
    if data["type"] == "progress":
        step = data["data"]["value"]
        total = data["data"]["max"]
        print(f"Progress: {step}/{total}")
    elif data["type"] == "executed":
        print(f"Node executed: {data['data']['node']}")
    elif data["type"] == "execution_complete":
        print("Generation complete!")

ws = websocket.WebSocketApp(
    "ws://127.0.0.1:8188/ws?clientId=agent-001",
    on_message=on_message
)
ws.run_forever()
```

## 6. Error Recovery Procedures

### 6.1 ComfyUI Crash / Unresponsive

**Symptoms**: HTTP timeout on `:8188`, WebSocket disconnection, no queue progress.

```powershell
# 1. Check if the process is alive
Get-Process -Name "python" | Where-Object { $_.CommandLine -like "*comfyui*" }

# 2. Kill any zombie processes
Get-Process -Name "python" | Where-Object { $_.CommandLine -like "*comfyui*" } | Stop-Process -Force

# 3. Clear the ComfyUI temp/cache if needed
Remove-Item "$env:TEMP\comfyui_*" -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

# 4. Restart ComfyUI
Start-Process -FilePath "python" -ArgumentList "main.py --listen 127.0.0.1 --port 8188 --preview-method auto" -WorkingDirectory "D:\ComfyUI" -NoNewWindow

# 5. Wait for startup and verify
Start-Sleep -Seconds 15
try {
    Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://127.0.0.1:8188/system_stats" -TimeoutSec 10
    Write-Host "ComfyUI recovered successfully" -ForegroundColor Green
} catch {
    Write-Host "ComfyUI failed to restart вҖ” check GPU/VRAM" -ForegroundColor Red
}
```

### 6.2 Out of Memory (OOM)

**Symptoms**: `CUDA out of memory` in logs, generation hangs, GPU VRAM at 100%.

```powershell
# 1. Check current VRAM usage
nvidia-smi

# 2. Clear ComfyUI's VRAM cache via API
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8188/free -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"unload_models": true, "free_memory": true}'

# 3. If still OOM, restart ComfyUI (see 6.1)

# 4. Reduce generation parameters for next attempt:
#    - Lower resolution (e.g., 1024x1024 instead of 2550x3300)
#    - Reduce batch_size to 1
#    - Use --lowvram or --medvram flag on restart
```

**Prevention Strategies:**
- Never queue more than 1 image at a time for high-resolution (2550x3300) generations
- Monitor VRAM between generations
- Use `--medvram` flag if GPU has вүӨ 8GB VRAM

### 6.3 Partial Generation (Incomplete Batch)

**Symptoms**: Some images generated, others missing. Batch shows as incomplete.

```python
# Check which images from a batch actually exist
import os
import json

batch_id = "batch_20260614_001"
expected_count = 24
output_dir = r"D:\AI-Business\output\kdp\images"

# Load generation history
with open(r"D:\AI-Business\logs\generation_history.jsonl") as f:
    history = [json.loads(line) for line in f if batch_id in line]

generated = [h for h in history if h.get("status") == "success"]
failed = [h for h in history if h.get("status") == "failed"]

print(f"Expected: {expected_count}")
print(f"Generated: {len(generated)}")
print(f"Failed: {len(failed)}")
print(f"Missing: {expected_count - len(generated) - len(failed)}")

# Re-queue only the missing/failed items
for item in failed:
    print(f"  Re-queue: {item['prompt_id']} вҖ” {item.get('error', 'unknown')}")
```

### 6.4 Corrupt Output File

**Symptoms**: Image file exists but is 0 bytes, truncated, or can't be opened.

```python
from PIL import Image
import os

output_dir = r"D:\AI-Business\output\kdp\images"
corrupt = []

for f in os.listdir(output_dir):
    filepath = os.path.join(output_dir, f)
    if not f.endswith(".png"):
        continue

    # Check file size
    if os.path.getsize(filepath) < 1024:  # Less than 1KB is suspicious
        corrupt.append((filepath, "too_small"))
        continue

    # Try to open
    try:
        img = Image.open(filepath)
        img.verify()
    except Exception as e:
        corrupt.append((filepath, str(e)))

if corrupt:
    print(f"Found {len(corrupt)} corrupt files:")
    for path, reason in corrupt:
        print(f"  вқҢ {path} вҖ” {reason}")
        # Optionally delete and re-queue
        # os.remove(path)
else:
    print("All files are valid вң…")
```

### 6.5 Ollama Model Not Responding

```powershell
# 1. Check model status
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags | python -m json.tool

# 2. Pull/reload the model if missing
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/pull -d '{"name": "llama3"}'

# 3. Test with a simple prompt
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate -d '{"model":"llama3","prompt":"ping","stream":false}'

# 4. If still failing, restart Ollama
Stop-Process -Name "ollama" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Start-Process -FilePath "ollama" -ArgumentList "serve" -NoNewWindow
Start-Sleep -Seconds 10
```

```powershell
    $name, $url = $_.Split("|")
    try {
```

## 8. Common Failure Modes and Fixes

| #  | Failure Mode                          | Symptoms                                              | Root Cause                                   | Fix                                                                                  |
|----|---------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1  | ComfyUI returns 500 on `/prompt`      | HTTP 500, `node_errors` in response                   | Invalid workflow JSON or missing node/model   | Validate workflow JSON, ensure all custom nodes installed, check model paths           |
| 2  | CUDA OOM during generation            | `CUDA out of memory` in logs, generation stalls       | Image too large, VRAM fragmented, batch > 1   | Free VRAM via API, reduce resolution, set batch_size=1, restart with `--medvram`      |
| 3  | Ollama returns empty response         | `response: ""` in JSON                               | Model not fully loaded, prompt too short      | Wait 30s after model load, re-send prompt, check model integrity with `ollama show`   |
| 4  | Black or blank images generated       | PNG exists but is solid black/white                   | Wrong VAE, empty prompt, bad sampler config   | Check VAE setting, verify positive prompt not empty, test with known-good workflow     |
| 5  | PDF assembly fails                    | Exception in `pdf_assembler.py`, partial PDF          | Missing images, corrupt PNG, wrong dimensions | Run corrupt file check (6.4), verify all images exist, check dimensions match spec    |
| 6  | Listing text is nonsensical           | Garbled or off-topic text from Ollama                 | Wrong model, temperature too high, bad prompt | Switch model, lower temperature to 0.7, review prompt template                        |
| 7  | WebSocket disconnects mid-generation  | WS close event, no progress updates                   | ComfyUI crashed or network timeout            | Reconnect WS, check ComfyUI status, poll `/history` as fallback                      |
| 8  | Lock file prevents new batch          | `*.lock` file blocking pipeline start                 | Previous run crashed without cleanup          | Check lock file age; if > 1h, delete it safely                                       |
| 9  | Disk full during generation           | Write errors, 0-byte output files                     | Output dir filled up, temp files accumulated  | Clean old outputs, clear temp dirs, check `D:\` free space                            |
| 10 | Model file corrupted                  | ComfyUI fails to load checkpoint, hash mismatch       | Incomplete download, disk error               | Re-download model, verify SHA256 hash, check disk health                              |
| 11 | Ollama connection refused             | `connection refused` on `:11434`                      | Ollama service not started                    | Run `ollama serve`, check if port is in use by another process                        |
| 12 | Images have wrong aspect ratio        | Output is square instead of 8.5x11                    | EmptyLatentImage dimensions wrong in workflow | Fix width/height in workflow (2550x3300 for 300DPI letter, or scale accordingly)      |
| 13 | ComfyUI queue stuck                   | Items in queue but nothing executing                  | GPU deadlock, paused execution                | Clear queue via API, restart ComfyUI, check GPU processes                             |
| 14 | Slow generation (>60s per image)      | High latency, low GPU utilization                     | CPU fallback, wrong device, thermal throttle  | Check `nvidia-smi` for GPU usage, verify CUDA is available, check thermals            |

## 9. Resumption Protocol

When you are an AI agent picking up this project, follow these steps **in order**:

### Step 1: Read This Document
You are here. вң…

### Step 2: Run System State Checklist
Execute the [Quick Validation Script](#quick-validation-script) from Section 1. **Do not proceed if any check fails.** Fix issues using the [Error Recovery Procedures](#6-error-recovery-procedures) first.

### Step 3: Check Last Known State

```powershell
# What was the last generation?
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\logs\generation_history.jsonl" -Tail 5 | ConvertFrom-Json | Format-Table

# Any incomplete batches?
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\logs\orchestrator.log" -Tail 50 | Select-String "batch|BATCH"

# Last error?
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\logs\error.log" -Tail 5
```

### Step 4: Review Configuration

```powershell
# Current settings
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\config\settings.yaml"

# Current niche targets
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\config\niches.yaml"
```

### Step 5: Run Health Checks
Execute the [Full Health Check](#full-health-check) from Section 7.

### Step 6: Clear Any Stale State

```powershell
# Remove stale lock files older than 1 hour
Get-ChildItem "D:\AI-Business\" -Recurse -Filter "*.lock" |
    Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt (Get-Date).AddHours(-1) } |
    Remove-Item -Force -Verbose

# Clear ComfyUI queue if stuck
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8188/queue -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"clear": true}'
```

### Step 7: Determine Next Action

Decision tree:

1. **Incomplete batch exists?** вҶ’ Resume the batch, re-queue only missing images
2. **All batches complete, PDFs not assembled?** вҶ’ Run PDF assembly
3. **PDFs assembled, listings not written?** вҶ’ Run listing generation
4. **Everything complete for current niche?** вҶ’ Move to next niche in queue
5. **No niches queued?** вҶ’ Run niche research to identify next profitable niche
6. **Manual task flagged?** вҶ’ Check `D:\AI-Business\logs\manual_tasks.txt` for operator notes

### Step 8: Begin Work

```powershell
# Start the orchestrator
python D:\AI-Business\scripts\orchestrator.py --resume

# Or start a specific stage
python D:\AI-Business\scripts\orchestrator.py --stage generate --niche "animals"
python D:\AI-Business\scripts\orchestrator.py --stage assemble --batch "batch_20260614_001"
python D:\AI-Business\scripts\orchestrator.py --stage listings --batch "batch_20260614_001"
```

### Step 9: Monitor Progress

```powershell
# Tail the orchestrator log
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\logs\orchestrator.log" -Wait -Tail 20

# Watch for errors
Get-Content "D:\AI-Business\logs\error.log" -Wait -Tail 10
```

### Step 10: Verify Outputs

```python
# Post-run verification
import os

kdp_dir = r"D:\AI-Business\output\kdp\pdfs"
etsy_dir = r"D:\AI-Business\output\etsy\images"

kdp_count = len([f for f in os.listdir(kdp_dir) if f.endswith(".pdf")]) if os.path.exists(kdp_dir) else 0
etsy_count = len([f for f in os.listdir(etsy_dir) if f.endswith((".png", ".jpg"))]) if os.path.exists(etsy_dir) else 0

print(f"KDP PDFs ready: {kdp_count}")
print(f"Etsy images ready: {etsy_count}")
```

# рҹҸ—пёҸ Technical Architecture Document

**Platform**: AI-Powered Passive Income Generator  
**Products**: KDP Coloring Books В· Etsy Wall Art  
**Core Stack**: ComfyUI В· Ollama В· Python В· PDF Assembly  
**Last Updated**: 2026-06-14

---

## Table of Contents

1. [System Overview](#1-system-overview)
2. [Component Diagram](#2-component-diagram)

## 1. System Overview

This platform is a fully automated content-generation pipeline that produces two revenue streams:

| Product           | Platform | Description                                          |
|-------------------|----------|------------------------------------------------------|
| **Coloring Books**| Amazon KDP | 24вҖ“50 page coloring books assembled as print-ready PDFs |
| **Wall Art**      | Etsy     | High-resolution digital art prints in multiple sizes  |

### High-Level Architecture

The system follows a **pipeline architecture** with six major subsystems:

1. **Niche Research** вҖ” Uses Ollama to analyze trends and select profitable niches
2. **Prompt Generation** вҖ” Ollama generates image prompts tailored to each niche
3. **Image Generation** вҖ” ComfyUI renders images from prompts using Stable Diffusion + LoRAs
4. **Post-Processing** вҖ” Python scripts resize, enhance, and validate output images
5. **Product Assembly** вҖ” PDF assembly for KDP books; multi-size export for Etsy art
6. **Listing Generation** вҖ” Ollama writes SEO-optimized titles, descriptions, and tags

All components run **locally** on a single Windows machine with NVIDIA GPU.

### Detailed Data Flow Steps

1. **Niche Selection** вҶ’ Ollama analyzes market data, competition levels, and search volume to identify profitable niches. Outputs a ranked list of niches with themes and keywords.

2. **Prompt Generation** вҶ’ For each niche, Ollama generates a batch of image prompts. Each prompt includes positive text (subject, style, modifiers) and negative text (anti-patterns). Prompt templates in `D:\AI-Business\templates\prompts\` guide the LLM output.

3. **Image Generation** вҶ’ ComfyUI receives workflow JSON populated with prompts. Each image is generated via the Stable Diffusion pipeline with appropriate LoRA weights. Raw PNG outputs are saved with metadata.

4. **Post-Processing** вҶ’ Raw images are validated (dimensions, color profile, file integrity), resized to target specifications, enhanced (contrast, sharpness for coloring pages; color correction for wall art), and saved in final format.

5. **PDF Assembly** (KDP only) вҶ’ Validated coloring page images are assembled into a print-ready PDF with correct page order, margins, bleed areas, and cover page. Cover is generated separately with title text overlay.

6. **Listing Writing** вҶ’ Ollama generates marketplace-optimized copy: titles (SEO-rich), descriptions (benefit-focused), bullet points, tags/keywords, and backend keywords for KDP.

7. **Output Packaging** вҶ’ All assets for each product are organized into upload-ready packages: PDFs + metadata for KDP, multi-size images + mockups + listing text for Etsy.

## 5. File Naming Conventions

### Images

```
{product_type}_{niche}_{sequence:03d}_{seed}.png
```

| Component       | Format          | Example                           |
|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------------------------|
| Product type    | `coloring` / `wallart` | `coloring`                  |
| Niche           | snake_case      | `ocean_animals`                   |
| Sequence        | 3-digit padded  | `001`, `024`                      |
| Seed            | integer         | `42`, `98765`                     |

**Examples:**
```
coloring_ocean_animals_001_42.png
coloring_ocean_animals_002_1337.png
wallart_floral_roses_001_55555.png
wallart_abstract_geometric_015_12345.png
```

### PDFs

```
{niche}_{book_type}_{date}_{variant}.pdf
```

**Examples:**
```
ocean_animals_interior_20260614_v1.pdf
ocean_animals_cover_20260614_v1.pdf
wild_safari_interior_20260614_v1.pdf
```

### Listings

```
{product_type}_{niche}_{platform}_{date}.json
```

**Examples:**
```
coloring_ocean_animals_kdp_20260614.json
wallart_floral_roses_etsy_20260614.json
```

### Batch Identifiers

```
batch_{YYYYMMDD}_{sequence:03d}
```

**Examples:**
```
batch_20260614_001
batch_20260614_002
```

## 9. Pipeline Stages

### Stage 1: Niche Research

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ NICHE RESEARCH                              в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Script:   niche_researcher.py               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Input:    Market data, previous niches      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Output:   niches.yaml updates               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Duration: 2вҖ“5 minutes                       в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Process:                                    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  1. Query Ollama for trending niche ideas   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  2. Score each niche (competition, demand)  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  3. Filter by profitability threshold       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  4. Generate sub-themes and keywords        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  5. Write selected niches to config         в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Error Handling:                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Ollama timeout вҶ’ retry 3x with backoff  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Empty response вҶ’ use fallback niche list в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Duplicate niche вҶ’ skip and log warning   в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Retry Logic:                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Max retries: 3                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Backoff: exponential (2s, 4s, 8s)        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Fallback: predefined safe niche list     в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Stage 2: Prompt Generation

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ PROMPT GENERATION                           в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Script:   ollama_bridge.py                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Input:    Niche config, prompt templates    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Output:   List of (positive, negative) pairsв”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Duration: 1вҖ“3 minutes per batch             в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Process:                                    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  1. Load niche definition and themes        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  2. Load prompt template for product type   в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ     a. Send template + niche to Ollama      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ     b. Parse response into structured promptв”Ӯ

в”Ӯ     d. Add to prompt batch                  в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ  5. Save prompt batch to file               в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Error Handling:                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Malformed response вҶ’ re-query with hint  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Offensive content вҶ’ filter and regenerateв”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Token limit exceeded вҶ’ truncate safely   в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Retry Logic:                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Max retries: 3 per prompt                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Backoff: linear (2s, 4s, 6s)             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҖў Fallback: generic prompt for niche       в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

```

```
в”Ӯ Process:                                    в”Ӯ
```

## 10. Concurrency Model

### Overview

The orchestrator uses a **single-threaded event loop with async I/O** pattern. Only one GPU-bound task (image generation) runs at a time, while CPU-bound and I/O-bound tasks can overlap.

### Concurrency Rules

| Resource          | Max Concurrent | Reason                                     |
|-------------------|---------------|---------------------------------------------|
| ComfyUI generation| **1**         | GPU VRAM is exclusive; parallel = OOM       |
| Ollama generation | **1**         | Shares GPU; concurrent = slow / OOM         |
| Post-processing   | **4**         | CPU-bound PIL operations, parallelizable    |
| PDF assembly      | **1**         | Memory-intensive, sequential page insertion |
| File I/O          | **8**         | Disk I/O bound, parallelizable              |
| Listing writing   | **1**         | Sequential to manage Ollama load            |

### Task Scheduling

```python
# Simplified orchestrator task scheduling
import asyncio
from asyncio import Semaphore

gpu_semaphore = Semaphore(1)      # Only 1 GPU task at a time
ollama_semaphore = Semaphore(1)   # Only 1 Ollama task at a time
cpu_semaphore = Semaphore(4)      # Up to 4 CPU tasks
io_semaphore = Semaphore(8)       # Up to 8 I/O tasks

async def generate_image(prompt, workflow):
    async with gpu_semaphore:
        # Only one image generation at a time
        result = await comfyui_bridge.queue_and_wait(prompt, workflow)
        return result

async def generate_text(prompt):
    async with ollama_semaphore:
        # Only one text generation at a time
        result = await ollama_bridge.generate(prompt)
        return result

async def post_process(image_path):
    async with cpu_semaphore:
        # Up to 4 concurrent post-processing tasks
        result = await asyncio.to_thread(post_processor.process, image_path)
        return result
```

### Pipeline Overlap Strategy

While the GPU is generating image N, the CPU can post-process image N-1:

```
Time вҶ’
GPU:  [===Gen Image 1===][===Gen Image 2===][===Gen Image 3===]
CPU:                      [Post-proc 1]      [Post-proc 2]
I/O:                                   [Save 1]         [Save 2]
LLM:  [Prompt Gen 2,3...]
```

### Batch Processing Flow

```python
async def process_batch(niche, count):
    """Process a full batch: generate prompts, images, post-process, assemble."""

    # Phase 1: Generate all prompts (sequential, Ollama)
    prompts = await generate_all_prompts(niche, count)

    # Phase 2: Generate images one-by-one, post-process in parallel
    processed = []
    for i, prompt in enumerate(prompts):
        # Generate (GPU exclusive)
        raw_image = await generate_image(prompt, workflow)

        # Post-process can overlap with next generation
        task = asyncio.create_task(post_process(raw_image))
        processed.append(task)

    # Wait for all post-processing to complete
    results = await asyncio.gather(*processed)

    # Phase 3: Assemble PDF (sequential)
    pdf = await assemble_pdf(results, niche)

    # Phase 4: Generate listing (Ollama)
    listing = await generate_listing(niche, pdf)

    return {"pdf": pdf, "listing": listing, "images": len(results)}
```

### State Persistence

The orchestrator persists state to disk after each significant step, enabling crash recovery:

```
D:\AI-Business\state\
в”ңв”Җв”Җ current_batch.json        # Active batch metadata
в”ңв”Җв”Җ generation_progress.json  # Per-image generation status
в”ңв”Җв”Җ pipeline_state.json       # Overall pipeline position
в””в”Җв”Җ task_queue.json           # Pending tasks
```
